explicit_text: I was born in a refugee camp in Thailand in 1999. Life there was very hard and full of fear. We had to wait a month to get food, and even then, we had to pay with our own money, which was scarce. The refugee camp felt like a prison because we couldn't go anywhere freely without risking punishment.
implicit_text: I was born in a refugee camp in Southeast Asia in 1999. Life there was very hard and full of fear. We had to wait a month to get food, and even then, we had to pay with our own money, which was scarce. The refugee camp felt like a prison because we couldn't go anywhere freely without risking punishment.

explicit_text: In 2010, my parents decided to move to the United States for a better life. After passing the interview and waiting for weeks, we finally prepared for the journey. We prayed for a safe trip and were very excited to arrive. When we landed, I was scared but relieved to be in the U.S., starting a new chapter for our family.
implicit_text: In 2010, my parents decided to move to North America for a better life. After passing the interview and waiting for weeks, we finally prepared for the journey. We prayed for a safe trip and were very excited to arrive. When we landed, I was scared but relieved to be in the new country, starting a new chapter for our family.

explicit_text: Now that I am in the United States, I am a high school student at Washington Technology Magnet School in St. Paul, Minnesota. Attending this school has been an important part of my life as I adjust to living in America. It represents the new opportunities I have after leaving the refugee camp.
implicit_text: Now that I am in North America, I am a high school student at a technology-focused magnet school in St. Paul, Minnesota. Attending this school has been an important part of my life as I adjust to living in this new country. It represents the new opportunities I have after leaving the refugee camp.

explicit_text: After arriving in the United States, my family settled in St. Paul, Minnesota. This city is where I attend Washington Technology Magnet School. Living in St. Paul has been a big change from the refugee camp and the places we passed through on our journey.
implicit_text: After arriving in North America, my family settled in the capital city of Minnesota. This city is where I attend the technology magnet school. Living here has been a big change from the refugee camp and the places we passed through on our journey.

explicit_text: During our journey to the United States, we stopped in Mae Sot to rest. The place was crowded and the bathrooms smelled bad, which was uncomfortable. Near our hotel, there was tall grass and a big round lake by the gate. While there, people from the International Organization for Migration taught us how to live in America.
implicit_text: During our journey to North America, we stopped in a border town in western Thailand to rest. The place was crowded and the bathrooms smelled bad, which was uncomfortable. Near our hotel, there was tall grass and a big round lake by the gate. While there, people from an international migration agency taught us how to live in America.

explicit_text: In Mae Sot, the International Organization for Migration helped us prepare for life in America. They taught us important lessons about what to expect and how to live once we arrived. Their support was crucial during that part of our journey.
implicit_text: In that border town, an international migration agency helped us prepare for life in America. They taught us important lessons about what to expect and how to live once we arrived. Their support was crucial during that part of our journey.

explicit_text: When we finally landed in the United States, it was in Kentucky. The weather was rainy and windy, which was different from what I was used to. This was the first place I saw in America, marking the start of our new life.
implicit_text: When we finally landed in North America, it was in a state known for its horse racing and bluegrass music. The weather was rainy and windy, which was different from what I was used to. This was the first place I saw in the new country, marking the start of our new life.
